brief_document.title: Session persistence — JSONL file management and dual persistence
brief_document.purpose: Wire Norn's JsonlSink-backed EventStore into the assistant service so that session conversation history persists to disk and can be replayed on resume. Establish the dual persistence model: JSONL files for Norn's conversation continuity, PG events for the UI's history display. Both coexist independently.
brief_document.task:
Create a session file manager that handles path resolution, directory creation, EventStore construction for new/resume/fork modes, and JSONL file lifecycle. The session file path convention is {data_dir}/sessions/norn/{session_id}.jsonl. spawn_norn_loop calls this manager to obtain an EventStore before building the AgentBuilder. The existing PG persistence (persist_events) and Redis streaming (stream_deltas_to_redis) remain unchanged — they subscribe to the AssistantEvent broadcast channel.

Dependency setup: No new cargo dependencies. The norn crate (JsonlSink, EventStore, SessionEvent) and tempfile are already dependencies of meridian-services.

requirements.title: Session directory and path resolution
requirements.spec: The system SHALL resolve Norn session JSONL file paths as {data_dir}/sessions/norn/{session_id}.jsonl where data_dir is the server's configured data directory. THE SYSTEM SHALL create the sessions/norn/ directory if it does not exist. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use ~/.norn/ (norn-cli local convention).
acceptance: Session files are at {data_dir}/sessions/norn/{session_id}.jsonl
Directory is created automatically if missing
data_dir comes from server configuration (ServiceContext or ServerConfig)
Path resolution is a pure function testable without filesystem

requirements.title: EventStore for new sessions
requirements.spec: WHEN a new Norn session starts (SessionStartMode::New), THE SYSTEM SHALL create a JsonlSink at the session file path in write mode AND construct EventStore::with_sink(sink) with empty initial events.
acceptance: New session creates a fresh JSONL file
EventStore has no initial events
JsonlSink is in write (create) mode
The session file path follows the {session_id}.jsonl convention

requirements.title: EventStore for resumed sessions
requirements.spec: WHEN an existing Norn session resumes (SessionStartMode::Resume), THE SYSTEM SHALL read the JSONL file at the session path, deserialize SessionEvent lines into a Vec<SessionEvent>, create a JsonlSink in append mode for the same file, and construct EventStore::with_sink_and_events(sink, events). IF the JSONL file does not exist, THE SYSTEM SHALL fall back to creating a new session (EventStore::with_sink, no error).
acceptance: Resume reads existing JSONL and replays events
JsonlSink is in append mode (does not truncate existing content)
EventStore receives pre-populated events from the file
Missing file falls back to new session behavior (no error, clean start)
Corrupt lines are logged and skipped, not fatal
Truncated trailing line (incomplete JSON from power failure mid-write) is treated as corrupt and skipped
Empty file (0 bytes) falls back to new session behavior
File containing valid JSON that is not SessionEvent is treated as corrupt per line

requirements.title: EventStore for forked sessions
requirements.spec: WHEN a session is forked (SessionStartMode::Fork), THE SYSTEM SHALL copy the source session's JSONL file to a new path (new session ID), then construct an EventStore from the copy using the resume flow. IF the source file does not exist, THE SYSTEM SHALL create a new session instead.
acceptance: Fork copies source JSONL to {new_session_id}.jsonl
New file is independent from source (copy, not symlink)
EventStore is built from the copy with resume semantics
Missing source file falls back to new session

requirements.title: AgentBuilder receives EventStore
requirements.spec: WHEN spawn_norn_loop builds the AgentBuilder, THE SYSTEM SHALL call .session(store) with the EventStore obtained from the session file manager. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT call .session() with a bare EventStore::new() (the bug in make_norn_step_runner).
acceptance: AgentBuilder::session(store) is called with a JsonlSink-backed EventStore
EventStore::new() is never used for member-facing Norn sessions
Session events are written to disk as the agent runs

requirements.title: PG and Redis persistence unchanged
requirements.spec: The existing persist_events and stream_deltas_to_redis tasks SHALL continue to subscribe to broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent> without modification. WHILE a Norn session runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL have both JSONL persistence (Norn-internal) and PG/Redis persistence (AssistantEvent broadcast) active simultaneously.
acceptance: persist_events spawned for Norn sessions exactly as for Claude Runner sessions
stream_deltas_to_redis spawned for Norn sessions exactly as for Claude Runner sessions
Both subscribe to the same broadcast channel that the Norn session loop writes to
No modifications to persistence.rs or streaming.rs

brief_document.dependency_setup: No new cargo dependencies. The norn crate (JsonlSink, EventStore, SessionEvent) and tempfile are already dependencies of meridian-services.
boundaries: SHALL NOT modify persistence.rs or streaming.rs
SHALL NOT modify the EventStore or JsonlSink implementations in the norn crate
SHALL NOT modify the JSONL format — use Norn's existing SessionEvent serialization
SHALL NOT implement cleanup/garbage collection of old session files (future scope)
verification: New session creates a fresh JSONL file at the correct path
Resume reads an existing JSONL file and pre-populates the EventStore
Fork copies the source file and builds from the copy
Missing source file for resume/fork falls back to new session without error
PG events appear in assistant_session_events for Norn sessions
Redis stream receives delta events for Norn sessions

checklist.text: Norn sessions call AgentBuilder::session(store) with a JsonlSink-backed EventStore
Session resume reads existing JSONL file and creates EventStore::with_sink_and_events
New sessions create EventStore::with_sink (empty events, append-only sink)
Session fork copies source JSONL to new path, then resumes from copy
PG persistence (persist_events) continues unchanged — subscribes to AssistantEvent broadcast
NornSessionStore type encapsulates server-side session file operations (create, resume, fork)

stories.text: As an AI agent, I want my session to resume from where I left off so that I retain full conversation context across wakes without re-reading history.
As a developer, I want Norn session JSONL files at a predictable path so that I can inspect conversation history for debugging.
As the system, I want Norn sessions to persist to both JSONL and PG so that session resume and UI history are independently served.

constraints.text: No new external dependencies. Namespace tools call service-layer functions via ServiceContext, not HTTP.
AssistantEvent is the single event type for all downstream consumers. Both runtimes produce it.
JSONL session files are Norn's source of truth for resume. PG is the UI's source of truth. Neither replaces the other.
The model sees at most 25 tools (16 standard Norn + up to 7 namespace + 2 optional web). Profile filtering reduces this in practice.
Session IDs are UUIDs shared between the JSONL filename, PG assistant_session_events key, and SessionHandle registry key.
Wake prompt delivery mechanism changes, format does not.
mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and re-exports.
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace).
All errors handled via thiserror in library code. No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code.
Namespace tools implement the existing Tool trait. No changes to the trait interface.

resolved_context.intention: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude Runner stays only for legacy background task dispatch until it can be retired entirely. Member-facing sessions run as typed, observable, in-process agent loops rather than opaque subprocess invocations. Session state persists across visits without relying on a CLI binary's internal persistence. The model sees a focused set of domain tools instead of a raw shell with 175 CLI commands. This is the June 15 deadline work.

provenance.quote: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude R
